pattern: Count elements strictly below k — each must be removed once, and order of removal doesn't matter because we always remove the current minimum.
hints: Each element less than k will eventually be the minimum at the time of its removal, so it must be removed.
Elements already >= k never need to be removed.
The answer is simply the count of elements in nums that are strictly less than k.

edge_inputs.description: all elements already >= k, zero operations needed
all but one element below k
k is the largest element, only that one survives
element equal to k does NOT need removal
k is 1 but array has 1s — 1 >= 1 so zero ops

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10**9) for _ in range(size)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 10**9)
    # Ensure at least one element >= k
    nums[rng.randint(0, size - 1)] = k + rng.randint(0, 10**8)
    return [nums, k]
